chapter_title.en: Chapter on a Man Borrowing to Perform Hajj and the Obligation of Hajj on Someone in Debt

text: 2902 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَلْمَلِكِ بْنِ عُتْبَةَ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ عَلَيْهِ دَيْنٌ يَسْتَقْرِضُ وَ يَحُجُّ قَالَ «إِنْ كَانَ لَهُ وَجْهٌ فِي مَالٍ فَلاَ بَأْسَ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2902 - It was narrated from Abdul-Malik ibn Utbah, who said:
I asked Abu al-Hasan (as) about a man who has debt but borrows money to perform Hajj.
Imam (as) said: "If he has a means to repay the debt, there is no harm in it."
